text: Implement recursive compression of old session summaries in wrapped-agents to bound memory usage
description: The compressOldSessions function in session-memory.ts has a TODO: use LLM call to compress N summaries into one meta-summary when over threshold. Currently just logs. Implement this to keep session memory bounded for long-running autonomy. Integrate call from watchdog or periodically. This improves supervisor reliability and supports cross-session learning in the ASI ideation aim.

status.comment: Core impl + wiring + test done; LLM call for richer summaries can be added in follow-up.

reflection: Implemented the TODO in compressOldSessions (heuristic meta-summary aggregation of outcomes/patterns/lessons/systemLimitations + prune old files; recursive). Wired the function: now called automatically from saveSummary after persisting each new session (non-fatal). Added passing unit test exercising create meta + prune when over threshold. Fresh type-check (tsc --noEmit clean) and full test suite (62/62 pass via npm test in common). This makes the previously dead compression actually bound memory for long-running supervisor sessions. Still room for real LLM meta-summary (e.g. via agent/MCP when called from loop).
